data.title: WebCatalog Tips: Best Ways to Turn Websites into Apps
data.excerpt: Learn how to use WebCatalog to turn any website into a desktop app. Manage multiple accounts and organize your workspace efficiently. Start now!

$media.alt: WebCatalog Tips: Best Ways to Turn Websites into Apps

children.text: Disclosure: This post may contain affiliate links, meaning I may earn a commission if you make a purchase through them, at no extra cost to you.

Ever find yourself with a dozen browser tabs open, each for a different web app or account? It’s chaotic. Your RAM cries for help, and finding the right window feels like a game of hide-and-seek. I’ve been there. That is, until I discovered a tool that completely changed how I interact with the web: WebCatalog. It’s a brilliant piece of software that lets you
turn any website into a dedicated desktop app
, and it’s become an essential part of my daily workflow.
If you're curious about
what WebCatalog is and how it works
, this guide will walk you through the best ways to use it, its standout features, and why it might be the productivity boost you didn't know you needed.
Why Choose WebCatalog Over Browser Tabs?

The first question is always "why?" Your browser already opens websites. The answer lies in focus, organization, and power. WebCatalog doesn't just create a simple shortcut. It builds a standalone application for each website you choose. This means each "app" gets its own window, its own icon in your dock or taskbar, and its own session.

The magic here is in account isolation. Need to manage two separate client accounts on the same platform? With WebCatalog, you can run them side-by-side, each in its own app, without ever logging out or using incognito modes. It’s perfect for freelancers, agencies, or anyone who juggles multiple identities online.
See why this makes WebCatalog a top choice for account management

Furthermore, WebCatalog cleans up your mental workspace. Instead of a messy browser with 30 tabs, you have a clean, organized dock of apps you actually use. You can group them by workflow—"Marketing," "Design," "Finance"—and switch contexts instantly. It’s a small change that yields a massive reduction in digital clutter.
New & Highlighted Features That Shine
WebCatalog is actively developed, and recent updates have added fantastic functionality. One of the most powerful is
Workspaces

. You can create multiple workspaces, each containing a different set of apps. Imagine having a "Deep Work" workspace with only your writing app, research tools, and music player, and a "Admin" workspace with email, project management, and social media schedulers. You can switch between these entire environments with a single click. It’s like having multiple, specialized desktops on demand.
Another standout feature is the
App Builder

. You aren’t limited to a preset list. If a website exists, you can turn it into an app. This includes internal company tools, niche SaaS products, or personal dashboards. The process is incredibly simple: just paste the URL, name your app, pick an icon, and you’re done.
Explore all the latest WebCatalog features in more detail here
For teams, the
Shared Spaces

feature (on certain plans) is a game-changer. You can curate and share a workspace configuration with colleagues. This ensures everyone has quick, consistent access to the core web tools your team relies on, eliminating the "what link was that?" problem on day one for new hires.
Best Ways to Use WebCatalog: Actionable Tips
Getting the most out of WebCatalog means thinking beyond just converting a few sites. Here are the best ways I’ve found to leverage its power.
1. Master Your Account Workflow.

This is the core superpower. I have separate WebCatalog apps for my personal Gmail, my business Gmail, and two client Gmail accounts. They all live in my dock, each with a distinct name and icon. I can glance at a specific app to see its unread count (if supported) and jump straight into the right context. The same goes for Slack, Trello, Asana, or any service where you manage multiple profiles. It eliminates the constant logout-login dance.
2. Build a Dedicated Command Center for Projects.

When starting a new major project, I create a WebCatalog Workspace just for it. Inside, I place the project management app (like ClickUp or monday.com), the relevant Google Drive folder, the Slack channel, and any specialized dashboards. This creates a focus zone. When I switch to that workspace, I’m in project mode—no distractions from other work.
Get inspired with more WebCatalog use cases for professionals
3. Turn Your Most-Used Sites into First-Class Citizens.

Some websites are so critical they deserve to be more than a bookmark. I’ve turned my password manager (1Password web), my note-taking app (Notion), and my calendar into standalone desktop apps via WebCatalog. They launch faster from my dock, integrate better with my OS (like appearing in Cmd+Tab), and feel like native applications.
4. Use It for Focus and Distraction Blocking.

This is an underrated tip. Create a Workspace that only contains your essential, productive apps. When you need to focus, switch to that workspace. Because WebCatalog apps are separate from your browser, you won’t be tempted to “just quickly check” a distracting tab. It’s a form of environmental design for your digital life.
For a step-by-step on setting up your first apps and workspaces, check out our
beginner’s guide to using WebCatalog
Who is WebCatalog Ideal For?
WebCatalog isn’t for absolutely everyone, but for the right person, it’s transformative. It’s ideal for:
Freelancers & Solopreneurs:
You wear many hats. WebCatalog lets you seamlessly switch between client portals, invoicing software, and your personal accounts without cross-contamination or mental load.
Remote Workers & Digital Nomads:
Your office is your laptop. Organizing your digital tools into distinct workspaces helps recreate the structure of a physical office, aiding concentration and reducing the feeling of disarray.
Social Media Managers & Marketers:
Managing multiple brand accounts on platforms like Twitter, Instagram, or Facebook is a breeze. Each account lives in its own app, with its own cookies and login state.
Developers & Designers:
Quickly turn documentation sites, staging servers, or design tool dashboards into persistent, easily accessible apps. Manage personal and client GitHub accounts with zero hassle.
Productivity Enthusiasts:
If you love optimizing your system, WebCatalog is a playground. You can experiment with workspace setups, app integrations, and notification management to build a truly personalized operating system.
Essentially, if you live in your browser and feel overwhelmed by tabs and accounts,
WebCatalog is built for you
. It’s a bridge between the chaotic web and the organized efficiency of native apps.
Getting Started Is Easier Than You Think
The learning curve is virtually flat. Head over to
WebCatalog.io

and download the app. The interface is clean and intuitive. To create your first app, click the “+” button, paste the website’s URL, give it a name, and select an icon. You can use the auto-fetched icon or upload your own for better organization.

Once you have a few apps, create your first Workspace by clicking the Workspace icon in the top-left. Drag and drop your new apps into it. That’s it. You’re now operating at a higher level of organization.

The free plan is quite generous, letting you create up to two apps. To unlock unlimited apps, workspaces, and shared spaces, the Pro plan is very reasonably priced for the constant updates and power it provides. It’s an investment in your daily focus and time.
Ready to declutter your digital life and command your web apps like a pro?
Try WebCatalog today and see the difference

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
Q: Is WebCatalog safe to use?
A:

Yes. WebCatalog is a legitimate application available on major platforms. It runs website apps in isolated sessions, similar to a browser, but as separate processes. It does not store your passwords; you log into each app as you normally would. Always download it from the official site.
Q: Does WebCatalog work on both Windows and Mac?
A:
Absolutely. WebCatalog is available for Windows, macOS, and Linux, making it a versatile choice for virtually any desktop environment.
Q: Can I use WebCatalog for free?
A:

WebCatalog offers a robust free tier that allows you to create two desktop apps. This is perfect for trying it out. For unlimited apps, access to Workspaces, Shared Spaces, and priority support, there is a Pro subscription.
Q: How is this different from just making a browser shortcut?
A:

A browser shortcut opens a site in a new browser tab. WebCatalog creates a standalone application window with its own data, cookies, and login session. This allows for true multi-account isolation, a separate process (which can help with resource management), and a dedicated icon in your system dock or taskbar.
